title: Machine-Verified SQG Shear-Vorticity Identity + Conditional Theorem 3 Roadmap (Lean 4)
description: Lean 4 + mathlib formalization of Fourier-space identities for the Surface Quasi-Geostrophic equation, working towards a machine-checked regularity proof. Covers Theorems 1 and 2 of paper D14 (shear-vorticity identity Ŝ_nt - ω̂/2 = |k|·sin²(α-β)·θ̂ and selection-rule bound) plus supporting Riesz/Sobolev infrastructure on the 2-torus. §10 adds a conditional Theorem 3 (SQG regularity) roadmap with named axiomatic hypotheses — MaterialMaxPrinciple, BKMCriterion, FracSobolevCalculus, SqgWellPosedness — that pin down exactly which analytic facts the regularity argument borrows from outside the algebraic layer. FracSobolevCalculus is discharged unconditionally. BKM's axiomatic scope is reduced to s > 1 only via mode-level Sobolev interpolation (the s ∈ [0, 1] range falls out from MaterialMaxPrinciple alone). §11-equivalent material derivative scaffolding introduces sqgVelocitySymbol, IsSqgVelocityComponent, and SqgEvolutionAxioms with real L²-conservation content. All content machine-verified with zero sorry across 7184 lines. Mathematical theorems by Bryan Sanchez (independent researcher); Lean formalization by Bryan Sanchez with AI assistance.
